rules: Any non-Automatic Semi-Automatic weapon is modified to become Fully Automatic, replacing Semi-Auto(X) with Auto (X).
If a weapon already has an Automatic firing mode, it can't receive this Modification.
To figure the weapon's new Rate of Fire, multiply Semi-Auto (X) by 2, then add +1. Example: Semi-Auto (3) becomes Auto (7).
The cost of this modification is the weapon's cost.
Any Covenant Carbine rifle can be modified to replace their 8.7x60mm CRP Round with a Subanese Blamite Crystal shard.
This upgrade gives the weapon the Needle (5) Special Rule.
The weapon must be reloaded with Needle Rifle Blamite Crystals and no longer works with the 8.7x60mm Rounds.
The weapon's Rate of Fire is increased by 50% of its original Rate of Fire.
If the weapon has Auto (6), it is increased to Auto (9).
If 50% is not enough to increase the Rate of Fire by 1, it increases the Rate of Fire by 1.
Price is +20 cR for every +1 added to the Rate of Fire.
Explosive Launcher weaponry are compatible with the Radiance Modification.
This Modification increases the distance in which Blast and Kill radius reach.
For every 4 meters the weapon's Blast reaches, it gains an extra +1 meter to that range.
For every 3 meters the weapon's Kill reaches, it gains an extra +1 meter to that range.
If either Blast or Kill are below the number needed to be increased by Radiance, they are increased by 1 meter.
The price of this modification is the weapon's original cost, multiplied by 2.
Any Plasma-based weapon can gain the Twin Attendants modification.
This modification is highly unstable, but for every shot the weapon would make, it makes two instead.
Each shot made takes double the magazine cost. Each shot fired needs to be Evaded separately.
After every Round, the weapon gains a 2% chance of overheating for every shot taken that turn.
If the weapon overheats, it gains the Overheat (X) Special Rule where (X) is how many shots were fired that Round.
The price of this modification is the weapon's original cost multiplied by 2.
Any Plasma-based weapon can gain the Slumber modification.
The weapon is fitted with fission-induced plasma cyclers.
On impact, Slumber Plasma reaches near-critical plasma fission causing a blast of energy.
Each time a character is struck with this, they are pushed back 1 meter.
The weapon's Pierce is reduced by 1/4th of its original Pierce.
The price of this modification is the weapon's Rate of Fire multiplied by 10.
Adds a Jiralhanae Combat Knife as a bayonet to the weapon.
This modification is not illegal within the Covenant, but is looked down upon by all races except Jiralhanae.
The Jiralhanae will also look down on Sangheili characters who use it, dropping the Disposition of these characters towards the user by two.
Weighs 1.4 kg.
A Smartlink-capable scope can be added to Covenant ranged weapons.
This scope can have up to two magnifications that can be switched as a Reaction, or as a Half Action to not receive a Reaction Penalty.
The price of the scope is the magnification in cR multiplied by 2. Magnification can't surpass 12x.
The scope upgrade installs it into the weapon and replaces any existing scope; it does not add a physical scope.
If purchasing a dual-magnification scope, the price is the highest magnification plus half (rounding up) of the lower magnification, multiplied by 2.
Example: A 4x/10x scope costs 24 cR (10 + 2, multiplied by 2).
The energy blade's color changes to a purple, red, or white.
The weapon rolls Special Damage on a 9 or 10.
An Unstable blade can't cloak with the character using it.
The energy blade's length is increased, giving the weapon +1 meter to Melee Reach.
The weapon is outfitted with a protective hand guard.
This gives the character's hand holding the weapon a +5 Bonus to Armor.
This hand guard also gives the character a +10 Bonus to defending against being Disarmed.
The energy blade glows incredibly bright and has a less stable magnetic-field generator, causing the blade to warp and send out arcs of plasma when the weapon is used in an Attack.
Other characters within a meter of the target must make an Evasion Roll or also be damaged by the energy blade's plasma arcs.
This splash damage does not figure in the energy blade's Pierce when damaging the other characters.
This modification also gives others a +10 to Visual Perception Tests to spot the weapon.
The weapon is made 25% lighter.
Due to the lighter-weight modification, the weapon also glows dully and deals less Pierce overall (-4 Pierce).
The weapon is made 25% heavier and is made nearly invisible to the naked eye unless you are up close to it.
This gives characters a -30 Penalty to Visual Perception Tests to spot the weapon.
The weapon is given a -10 Penalty to Parrying and a -10 Penalty to being Parried.
The weapon is built with an Active Camo cloaking system and counts as the character using it when held.
The weapon and the user are both cloaked when activated.
Serrated edges are added to the middle of each side of the energy sword.
These are designed to allow the weapon to catch and parry, giving a +10 Bonus to Parrying.
The large blade of the Gravity Hammer is removed and replaced with powerful thrusters.
When the hammer's Gravity effect is activated, the hammer gains a +10 Bonus to Base Damage to that Attack.
The hammer is larger and bulkier. The hammer weighs an extra 12 KG and is given a +1 meter to Melee Reach.
The weapon also gains Gravity (5) instead of Gravity (4).
The blade on the Gravity Hammer is increased in size, similar to a Battle Axe.
This gives the blade of the weapon -3 to Damage and +10 to Pierce.
This increases the weight of the hammer by 3 KG.
The hammer is given a longer handle. This gives the weapon a +1 meter to Melee Reach.
The weapon is outfitted with a protective hand guard.
This gives the character's hand holding the weapon a +5 Bonus to Armor.
This hand guard also gives the character a +10 Bonus to defending against being Disarmed.
The hammer's length is reduced, reducing the weight of the hammer by 17 KG.
This also reduces the weapon's Melee Reach by -1 meter.
The hammer's Gravity Special Rule no longer hits as a radius, but instead strikes as if it were a Flame (2D10) effect.
The Flame (2D10) effect reaches out to 8 meters long and 2 meters wide when activated.
The hammer loses the Gravity Special Rule and is replaced with the Gravimetric (10) Special Rule.
The hammer's blade is removed, but the weapon is given 4 plasma blades.
This gives the hammer +12 to Pierce and +4 KG to the hammer's weight.
Cauterize Special Rule.
